evals.name: Scenario Creation and Terrain
evals.description: Creates a scenario with GMTED2010 terrain and verifies it initializes
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario centered at Boston (lat 42.355, lon -71.066) with 10Hz update rate and 20s stop time. Add GMTED2010 terrain for a 500m x 500m area using local ENU coordinates. Call setup and verify the scenario is ready by printing the CurrentTime. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

assertions.description: Code runs without error
Scenario uses ReferenceLocation in constructor
assertions.expected: uavScenario constructor includes ReferenceLocation name-value pair set to [42.355 -71.066 0] — NOT created without a reference location

assertions.description: Uses addMesh with terrain type and gmted2010
assertions.expected: addMesh(scene, "terrain", {"gmted2010", xLim, yLim}, color) — NOT addMesh without color argument, NOT missing the geometry cell

assertions.description: Does not use UseLatLon when prompt specifies local ENU
assertions.expected: "UseLatLon"

evals.name: Quadrotor Trajectory Flight
evals.description: Creates a quadrotor on a waypoint trajectory and runs the simulation loop to completion
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (10Hz, 15s) at lat 42.355, lon -71.066. Add a quadrotor platform named 'Quad1' flying a rectangular path at 40m altitude in NED frame with 5 waypoints. Run the simulation loop and print the final platform position after the loop completes. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Simulation loop runs to completion without error
waypointTrajectory uses NED ReferenceFrame
assertions.expected: waypointTrajectory includes "ReferenceFrame", "NED" — NOT ENU, NOT omitted (default is ENU which would mismatch the platform)

assertions.description: updateMesh uses orientation [0 1 0 0] for NED
assertions.expected: updateMesh call uses quaternion [0 1 0 0] (180-degree roll) — NOT [1 0 0 0] (identity, which inverts the mesh in NED)

assertions.description: Does not use invalid multirotor mesh type
assertions.expected: "multirotor"

evals.name: GPS Sensor via uavSensor Adaptor
evals.description: Attaches a GPS sensor to a platform and collects readings through the simulation loop
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (10Hz, 10s) at lat 37.7749, lon -122.4194. Add a quadrotor flying a straight line from [0 0 -30] to [100 0 -30] in NED. Attach a GPS sensor using the uavSensor adaptor pattern. Run the simulation loop, call updateSensors each step, collect GPS position readings, and print the number of GPS updates received. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Code runs and produces GPS readings count
Uses uavSensor adaptor, not standalone gpsSensor
assertions.expected: uavSensor("GPS", plat, gps, ...) constructor binds the sensor to the platform — NOT calling gpsSensor step() manually in the loop

assertions.description: Calls updateSensors(scene) inside advance loop
assertions.expected: updateSensors(scene) called inside the while advance(scene) loop, before read(sensor) — NOT omitted

assertions.description: read(sensor) uses isUpdated check
assertions.expected: [isUpdated, t, ...] = read(sensorObj) followed by if isUpdated guard — NOT reading without checking isUpdated flag

assertions.description: Does not manually step the GPS sensor
assertions.expected: step(gps

evals.name: INS Sensor Read Interface
evals.description: Attaches an INS sensor and correctly reads orientation as struct output
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (10Hz, 10s) at lat 42.355, lon -71.066. Add a quadrotor flying a curved path at 25m altitude. Attach an INS sensor using uavSensor adaptor. Run the simulation, read the INS data each step, extract roll/pitch/yaw from the orientation, and print the final attitude in degrees. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Code runs and prints attitude values
read(insSensorObj) returns 3 outputs: [isUpdated, t, struct]
assertions.expected: [isUpdated, t, insData] = read(insSensorObj) with exactly 3 outputs — NOT 7 separate outputs like [isUpdated, t, orient, pos, vel, acc, angvel]

assertions.description: Accesses orientation via struct field, not separate output
assertions.expected: insData.Orientation accessed as a quaternion object, then converted via euler() — NOT treating the 3rd output as a numeric orientation directly

evals.name: Lidar Sensor with Organized Output
evals.description: Attaches a lidar sensor and correctly handles organized MxNx3 point cloud
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (10Hz, 10s) at lat 42.355, lon -71.066. Add 2 polygon buildings (20m tall). Add a quadrotor flying between the buildings at 10m altitude. Attach a uavLidarPointCloudGenerator with HasOrganizedOutput=true via uavSensor. Run the simulation, collect minimum lidar distance to obstacles at each step, and print the overall minimum distance. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Code runs and prints minimum distance
Reshapes organized output before filtering
assertions.expected: reshape(ptCloud.Location, [], 3) called before applying logical indexing — NOT indexing the MxNx3 array directly with a 2D logical mask (which causes 'logical indices outside array bounds' error)

assertions.description: Filters NaN/Inf points before computing distance
assertions.expected: isfinite or ~isnan check applied to reshaped points before distance computation — NOT computing vecnorm on raw points containing NaN/Inf

assertions.description: Uses reshape to flatten organized point cloud
assertions.expected: reshape(

evals.name: Simulation Loop with show3D Visualization
evals.description: Runs a full simulation with animated 3D visualization using FastUpdate
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (5Hz, 10s) at lat 42.355, lon -71.066. Add GMTED2010 terrain (200m x 200m, local ENU). Add a quadrotor on a circular path (radius 50m, altitude 30m) with 30 waypoints. Visualize with show3D using FastUpdate in the simulation loop. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Animation loop completes without error
First show3D call creates axes before the loop
assertions.expected: ax = show3D(scene) called once before the while advance loop — NOT calling show3D without capturing axes, NOT omitting the initial call

assertions.description: Loop uses FastUpdate and Parent for efficient animation
assertions.expected: show3D(scene, "FastUpdate", true, "Parent", ax) inside the loop — NOT calling show3D without FastUpdate (which recreates the figure each frame)

assertions.description: Uses drawnow limitrate for smooth animation
assertions.expected: drawnow limitrate inside the loop — NOT drawnow without limitrate (causes unnecessary redraws), NOT omitting drawnow entirely (no visual update)

assertions.description: Uses FastUpdate name-value in loop show3D call
assertions.expected: FastUpdate

evals.name: Multi-UAV with Buildings
evals.description: Creates multiple UAV platforms with different trajectories in a building environment
evals.prompt: Create a uavScenario (10Hz, 20s) at lat 42.355, lon -71.066. Add 3 polygon buildings of different heights. Add 2 quadrotor platforms flying different paths at different altitudes so they don't collide. Run the simulation and print each platform's final position. Save the code as a .m script file and run it.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox required

assertions.description: Multi-platform simulation completes
updateMesh uses [0 1 0 0] orientation for NED platforms
assertions.expected: All updateMesh calls use quaternion [0 1 0 0] — NOT [1 0 0 0] (identity, which inverts mesh in NED frame)

assertions.description: Buildings use addMesh polygon with height extent
assertions.expected: addMesh(scene, "polygon", {vertices, [0 height]}, color) — vertices is Nx2 polygon, height extent is [0 H], color is RGB triplet — NOT missing the color argument, NOT using 3D vertices

assertions.description: Does not use invalid multirotor mesh type
assertions.expected: "multirotor"
